svg.text: comm_pe.csl — the decode block's communication toolbox
One collective shape (2-phase chain all-reduce + multicast broadcast), EIGHT payload extents, THREE axes. m=1 per step => every GEMM is a GEMV => P-1 everywhere, no Cannon, no shuttle.
Block = P_BLOCK_SIZE x P_BLOCK_SIZE (8x8 ref cfg). Y (rows) = HIDDEN dim shard AND KV-sequence shard. X (cols) = attention / FFN column width. kv-band = a run of X-PEs for one KV head.
branch lexu/staging/s6a-inner-pe-kv-route-a, uncommitted working tree · ref config test_sim_2x2block_kv_varlen.json
A. The four movement shapes decode actually uses
P-1 two-phase chain all-reduce (x6 payload variants)
phase1 c1/q3,c2/q4 -> phase2 c3/q5,c4/q6 -> bcast c5/q7
phase 1: reduce inside groups of pe_num_per_group -> root_1st_phase
phase 2: reduce among group roots -> root_2nd_phase (=5)
P-2 router multicast broadcast back (only the root sends)
Depth P/(2g)+g/2, minimized at g=sqrt(P). Same code, 8 extents:
bsz | bsz*dim_per_pe | QKV fusion | ZZ fusion | gqa_group (sum & max).
Fusion = two reductions sharing an axis ride ONE chain: 6 collectives/layer, not 9.
P-7 kv-head band reduce (single phase, FIXED root)
QK-Norm + Q.K^T score · c1/q3, c2/q4 + bcast c5/q7 · reduce_2nd untouched
AXIS FLIP vs prefill: prefill's kv band runs along Y, decode's along X.
Root is FIXED, so ONE color pair suffices — no cycling root, hence no
two-color-pair split and no enter_qkt_reduce. Colors 3,4 stay free for
phase 2. Entire reduce no-ops when pes_per_kv_head == 1 (counts stay exact).
P-6 inter-block handoff + P-2 intra-row fan-out
c19/OQ1, c20/OQ2 alternating by edge parity · fan-out c6, IQ2/OQ0
ONE synchronous @fmovh of bsz*dim_per_pe bf16 words
(solid = color a / dashed = color b, alternating by parity)
NO shift register, NO run_shuttle: the tile moves once, point-to-point.
Two globally-pinned ids (19/20) so a wavelet crosses the row_y boundary on the
same color; each PE binds BOTH queue pairs at comptime, picks by is_recv_on_a_rt.
G-14 KV-ingress borrows the broadcast queues (the one real drain)
IQ7/OQ7 <-> {broadcast c5} | {kv_ingress c17, c21} · S6a: now bidirectional
@queue_flush(OQ7) -> kv_ingress_oq_empty handler -> re-encode IQ7/OQ7 -> queue_flush.exit -> @activate continuation
Ingress colors REUSE ids 17/21 (kpipe_b k7 / UP_A) — disjoint rectangle:
those live on strip/HT_head cells, the KV west-shift on block-column cells.
Odd fabric COLUMNS swap the two colors, giving the west-shift its parity order.
B. Per-layer axis schedule — reconfig_allreduce_axis repaints the SAME five colors six times per layer, with NO fence
Race-free ONLY because every all_reduce_* is synchronous and ends in a multi-tx broadcast: per-child router backpressure makes the collective self-fencing (comm_pe.csl:1325-1334).
orange tick = a reconfig_allreduce_axis() repaint (6 per layer, incl. the trailing restore to Y for the next layer) · decode.csl:1424-1484
C. Color + queue binding map — every queue is bound (contrast prefill, which leaves q4 free)
inter_block_a recv — or x_input_color on is_x_receiver
chain-start block reads HT_head's post-embed X
runtime pick between IQ0/IQ1 via is_recv_on_a_rt
also K-pipe strip id (disjoint X cells)
also carries both P-7 band chains
broadcast_color — OR kv_ingress colors before the rebind
the only @queue_flush + empty-queue handler in the file
inter_block Z send (opposite color from recv)
layout-global ids: same physical id in every row_y region

ABSENT vs prefill: two_hop_comm, left_matrix_shift, attn_right_hop, the Score x V band Cannon, run_shuttle + the whole RECFG_SH_OUT/IN machine, the cycling-root Q.K^T split. Zero microthreads, zero async fabric ops.

Legend: red = phase-1 / Y axis purple = phase-2 blue = broadcast / kv-band green = X axis orange = inter-block + repaint. All fabric DSD extents comptime; runtime variation goes in the count, never the length.
